verdict_reasoning: B2M (beta-2 microglobulin) is constitutively displayed on the outer leaflet of virtually all nucleated cells as the non-covalent light-chain subunit of MHC class I (HLA) complexes. It has no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor of its own, but it is assembled intracellularly with the TM-anchored HLA heavy chain (α-chain) and co-trafficked as a stable complex to the cell surface, where it lies beside the α3 domain, fully extracellular. This meets the stable_complex_partner criterion: B2M is a constitutive, wash-resistant partner of a TM-anchored scaffold, assembled and co-trafficked as a complex. It is also targeted by anti-cancer surface-directed antibody programs. The equilibrium exchange with soluble serum B2M is a secondary phenomenon; the dominant biology is stable surface display on the MHC-I complex on essentially every nucleated cell.
